betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6RBMK Fuel Types & Mechanics§f

§aChoose your fuel wisely — each type has unique flux functions, heat profiles, and danger levels!§f

§6Fuel Assembly§f: 8 billets + Empty RBMK Fuel Rod = filled rod (shapeless recipe). Fuel rods go into Fuel Channels.

§6Key Stats§f:
§b• Yield§f — total neutron capacity (most fuels: §e100M§f, neutron sources: §e25–50M§f). Depleted by incoming flux each tick.
§b• Depletion§f — 0–100%, affects reactivity via decay function
§b• Flux Function§f — the math that determines output flux from input flux. §e8 types§f, ascending danger:

§7Passive§f — constant output. §oSafest.§f Used in neutron sources.
§7Euler§f — rapid growth to absolute max. §oVery safe.§f
§7Sigmoid§f — S-curve, slow → fast → plateau. §oSafe with care.§f
§7Logarithmic§f — grows but diminishing returns. §oSafe.§f
§7Square Root§f — responsive, soft cap. §oReasonably dangerous.§f
§7Linear§f — proportional output. §oVery dangerous§f — exponential feedback loop.
§7Negative Quadratic§f — peaks then lowers. §oExtremely dangerous.§f
§7Quadratic§f — runaway growth. §oIncomprehensibly dangerous.§f

§6Heat Per Flux§f — °C generated per unit of output flux. High values = rapid heating.
§6Diffusion§f — rate heat transfers from core to skin (0.0–1.0). Higher = faster meltdown risk.
§6Melting Point§f — skin temp limit before meltdown.

§6Fuel Danger Categories§f:

§aLow Danger§f: Ra226Be, Po210Be, ThMEU, LEAus, NU. Safe to operate with cooling.
§eMedium Danger§f: MEU, LEP239, MOX, MEN, HEN, LES, MEP239, HEU. Can melt down without control.
§cHigh Danger§f: AmZFB, HEU233, HEP239, HEP241, HEA242, MES, MEA, HES, Flashgold/lead, Balefire, Digamma. Extremely reactive, require strict control.

§6Self-Igniting Fuels§f: LEA, MEA, HEA241, LEAus, Pu238Be, Ra226Be, Po210Be, Flashgold, Flashlead, Balefire, Digamma. Produce heat even without external flux.

§6Decay Functions§f:
§b• Linear§f — steady decline to 0
§b• Gentle Slope§f — slight bump then slow decline
§b• Raising Slope§f — peaks at ~120% then steep drop
§b• Boosted Slope§f — dips, surges to ~135%, then crashes
§b• Static§f — no change

§eTip§f: For first RBMK, start with §bThMEU§f (safest) or §bMEU§f (easy to refine). Never put §bDigamma§f in a reactor with reflectors unless you want a hole in your world!
